title: Unify targetId format across all engines — use consistent 'element-{index}' for sorting
description: TYPE SMELL: VisualMutation.targetId is `string` with 7 different formats across categories. Sorting uses '0','1' (plain numbers), graph uses 'node-A', tree uses 'tnode-root', DP uses 'dp-2-3'. This is the ROOT CAUSE of P0-002 (parseStepMutations regex mismatch). Fix ALL sorting engines to use 'element-{index}' format matching what parseStepMutations expects. Consider adding a branded type: type TargetId = string & { __brand: 'TargetId' }. At minimum, document the format convention per category in types.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: All sorting engines use 'element-{index}' format for targetId
parseStepMutations regex matches the standard format
targetId format convention documented in types.ts as JSDoc comments
Graph/Tree/DP/String/Grid/Geometry formats documented (no change needed, they work)
Unit test validates targetId format per engine category

title: Replace AlgorithmResult.finalState with discriminated union per algorithm category
description: TYPE SMELL: finalState: number[] means 5 different things (sorted array, distances, MST weight, queen columns, DP row). Consumers must cast/guess. Replace with discriminated union: SortingResult { category:'sorting'; sortedArray:number[] }, GraphResult { category:'graph'; distances:Map<string,number> }, etc. Each engine returns its specific result type. The base AnimationStep[] + config remain shared. This eliminates an entire class of 'what does this number mean?' bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: AlgorithmResult is a discriminated union with category field as discriminant
Each category has a specific result type with meaningful field names
All 62 engine functions return their specific result type
AlgorithmPanel and AlgorithmModule consume result via category switch
TypeScript narrows the result type after checking category

title: Add literal union type for algorithm IDs — enable exhaustive runner coverage checks
description: TYPE SMELL: AlgorithmConfig.id is `string` — TypeScript can't verify that every config ID has a runner in SORTING_RUNNERS. This is why 24 algorithms silently fail. Change to: type SortingAlgoId = 'bubble-sort' | 'insertion-sort' | ... ; Make SORTING_RUNNERS: Record<SortingAlgoId, RunnerFn> — TypeScript will ERROR on any missing ID. Same for GRAPH_RUNNERS, TREE_RUNNER_IDS, etc. This makes the 'unwired runner' class of bugs IMPOSSIBLE.
acceptanceCriteria: SortingAlgoId, GraphAlgoId, TreeAlgoId, DPAlgoId, etc. are literal union types
AlgorithmConfig.id typed as AlgoId (union of all category IDs)
SORTING_RUNNERS typed as Record<SortingAlgoId, RunnerFn> — no missing keys allowed
Adding a new algorithm to SORTING_ALGORITHMS without a runner causes TypeScript error
handleRun uses exhaustive switch with 'never' default for compile-time coverage

title: Refactor AlgorithmModuleState from god-type to discriminated union per visualization type
description: TYPE SMELL: AlgorithmModuleState has 21 fields, 16 of which are category-specific nullables. When viewing sorting, 12 fields are null and irrelevant. Refactor to: type AlgorithmModuleState = SortingViewState | GraphViewState | TreeViewState | DPViewState | ... where each variant only has the fields relevant to that visualization type. The visualizationType field becomes the discriminant. This eliminates null-checking everywhere and makes invalid states unrepresentable.
acceptanceCriteria: AlgorithmModuleState is a discriminated union with visualizationType as discriminant
SortingViewState has only: currentArray, elementStates, comparison fields
GraphViewState has only: currentGraph, step fields
Each variant has exactly the fields needed — no nullable category-specific fields
useAlgorithmModule() hook preserves shared fields (currentStep, stepIndex, selectedAlgoId) in all variants

title: Extract shared BaseElementState and unify per-category state naming
description: TYPE SMELL: ElementState, GraphElementState, TreeElementState, GeometryElementState use different names for the same concepts. 'comparing' (sorting) = 'visiting' (graph) = 'current' (tree). This prevents creating shared visualization components. Extract a shared base: type BaseElementState = 'default' | 'active' | 'success' | 'error' | 'highlight'. Each category extends with specific states. Shared color mapping can then work with BaseElementState while category-specific visualizers add their own mappings.
acceptanceCriteria: BaseElementState type defined with 3-5 universal states
SortingElementState, GraphElementState, TreeElementState extend BaseElementState
A shared getBaseStateColor(state) works for common states across all visualizers
Per-category visualizers add their own color mapping for category-specific states
Documentation in types.ts explains the state naming convention

title: Add exhaustive switch in handleRun to make missing category handlers a compile error
description: TYPE SMELL: handleRun (AlgorithmPanel.tsx:389-605) uses if/else if chains for vizType. If a new category is added without a handler, there's no compile error — just a silent return. Add exhaustive switch: switch(vizType) { case 'array': ...; case 'graph': ...; ...; default: const _exhaustive: never = vizType; } TypeScript will ERROR if any visualizationType value is unhandled. This prevents the entire class of 'added to dropdown but no runner' bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: handleRun uses a switch statement on vizType instead of if/else chain
Default case has never assertion for exhaustiveness
Adding a new visualizationType to the union WITHOUT adding a switch case causes TypeScript error
All existing categories have their switch cases
getVisualizationType function also uses exhaustive switch
